source.title: Design and application of a lactulose biosensor

initial_request.research_problem: Lack of regulatory proteins for desired small molecules limits biosensor applications; existing lactulose biosensors are in vitro and not suitable for in vivo high-throughput screening.
initial_request.known_context: LacI is a well-characterized transcriptional regulator that responds to IPTG and native sugars; its effector-binding pocket can potentially be engineered for new specificities.
constraints: Must not respond to lactose or other common disaccharides
Must function in E. coli whole-cell context
quantifiable_goals: Enable high-throughput screening of lactulose-producing enzymes

evolution_trajectory.state_before: No regulatory protein available for lactulose detection; existing biosensors are in vitro.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Can LacI effector-binding pocket be engineered to recognize lactulose?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Engineering the effector specificities of regulatory proteins is an efficient way to develop customized small-molecule biosensors.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Proceed to design a saturation library targeting key residues in the LacI effector-binding pocket.
evolution_trajectory.observation: LacI is a suitable scaffold due to its well-characterized structure and effector-binding pocket.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Hypothesis supported by literature; design saturation library to test.

evidence.quote_or_span: the lack of available regulatory proteins that respond to small molecules of interest has limited the applications of these biosensors
Engineering the effector specificities of regulatory proteins is an efficient way to develop customized small-molecule biosensors

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Hypothesis formulated; need to create library to test LacI variants.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Which residues in the effector-binding pocket should be targeted?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Mutating residues I79, F161, N246, L296 will alter effector specificity.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Construct site-directed saturation library at these four positions using overlap extension PCR and MEGAWHOP cloning.

evidence.quote_or_span: LacI site-directed saturation library was constructed with overlap extension PCR method.
After four rounds of the dual screening, eight mutants displaying responses to lactulose were selected, among which the mutant LacI-L5, carrying one amino acid substitution (F161K), showed \~2.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Saturation library constructed; need to isolate lactulose-responsive variants.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How to screen for specific lactulose induction while eliminating leaky and lactose-responsive variants?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Dual FACS screening (negative without inducer, negative with lactose, positive with lactulose) will enrich desired mutants.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform four rounds of dual FACS screening.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Enrichment of lactulose-responsive clones after four rounds.

evidence.quote_or_span: This dual screening was repeated four times, followed by a final round of negative screening in the absence of inducer.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: FACS screening completed; eight mutants selected.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: What are the induction properties and specificity of the best mutant?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Mutant LacI-L5 (F161K) will show specific induction by lactulose.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Characterize LacI-L5 induction profile and specificity.
evolution_trajectory.observation: LacI-L5 shows ~2.7-fold induction by lactulose, half-maximal at 0.13 mM, and no response to other disaccharides.

evidence.quote_or_span: Half-maximal induction occurs at 0.13 mM lactulose.
almost no induction in LacI-L5
After four rounds of the dual screening, eight mutants displaying responses to lactulose were selected, among which the mutant LacI-L5, carrying one amino acid substitution (F161K), showed \~2.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: LacI-L5 characterized; need to confirm direct binding.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: SPR will show binding of lactulose to LacI-L5 but not wild-type LacI.
evolution_trajectory.observation: LacI-L5 binds lactulose with KD=17 mM; wild-type LacI shows no binding.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Binding confirmed, but affinity is weak; note limitation.

evidence.quote_or_span: After four rounds of the dual screening, eight mutants displaying responses to lactulose were selected, among which the mutant LacI-L5, carrying one amino acid substitution (F161K), showed \~2.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: LacI-L5 validated; need to assess remaining IPTG response.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Does LacI-L5 still respond to IPTG?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: LacI-L5 may retain some IPTG responsiveness.
evolution_trajectory.observation: LacI-L5 remained responsive to IPTG, though less than wild-type.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: IPTG response is a limitation; proceed to apply biosensor anyway.

evidence.quote_or_span: LacI-L5 remained responsive to IPTG induction, although to a less extent than wild-type LacI

evolution_trajectory.state_before: LacI-L5 biosensor characterized; need to engineer C2E for improved lactulose production.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Can the biosensor be used to screen a C2E mutant library?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Random mutagenesis of C2E will yield variants with enhanced lactulose production detectable by the biosensor.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Construct C2E random mutagenesis library via error-prone PCR and MEGAWHOP.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Library ready for screening with biosensor.

evidence.quote_or_span: The random mutagenesis library of C2E was constructed through error-prone PCR.
Ten randomly picked clones from the library were sequenced and contained an average of 3 nucleotide mutations per clone.
Construction of the random mutagenesis library of C2E.
Then the PCR products obtained, containing randomly mutated C2E gene, were used as megaprimer to perform MEGAWHOP PCR using plasmid pN25-C2E as template.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: C2E library constructed; need to screen for improved lactulose producers.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Which colonies show enhanced lactulose production?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Co-expression with LacI-L5 biosensor on lactose-containing agar will yield brighter GFP fluorescence in better producers.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Screen library on LB agar with 10 mM lactose; select colonies with stronger GFP fluorescence.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Selected colonies to be verified by HPLC.

evidence.quote_or_span: The C2E random mutagenesis library was transformed into the BLGR strain harboring the plasmid pLac7-L5.
The cells were then grown on LB agar plates containing 10 mM lactose.
mutant strains showing stronger GFP fluorescence than colonies expressing wild-type C2E were selected for rescreening in the test tubes.
(a) The BLGR strains co-expressing LacI-L5 and the C2E random mutagenesis library grown on LB agar supplemented with 10 mM lactose under UV 302 nm.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Fluorescent colonies selected; need to quantify lactulose production.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Do selected mutants actually produce more lactulose?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: HPLC will confirm enhanced lactulose production.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Quantify lactulose production by HPLC for selected variants.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Mutant C2 (E272D, F339S) shows ~22-fold increase in lactulose production at 37°C.

evidence.quote_or_span: The best of the variants, C2(E272D, F339S), displayed \~22-fold increase in lactulose production at 37°C (Fig.
(b) Lactulose productions of the selected variants expressing the C2E mutants at 37°C, compared with that of strain expressing the wild-type C2E (C2E-WT).
HPLC analyses verified enhanced lactulose production by these selected variants compared with that of the strain expressing wild-type C2E.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: C2 mutant identified; need to understand basis of improvement.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Is increased production due to higher expression or higher specific activity?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Western blot and specific activity assays will reveal the mechanism.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform Western blot and specific activity measurements.
evolution_trajectory.observation: ~32-fold higher expression of C2 vs wild-type; specific activity only 1.3-fold higher (11 U/mg vs 14 U/mg at 37°C).
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Expression increase is main driver; check codon bias.

evidence.quote_or_span: Western-blot analysis of mutant C2 expression as compared with the wild-type enzyme.
Specific activities of wild-type C2E (C2E-WT) and mutant C2 at 37°C and 80°C.
(d) Specific activities of wild-type C2E (C2E-WT) and mutant C2 at 37°C and 80°C.

1-fold at 37°C and 80°C (optimum reaction temperature of wild-type C2E), respectively, indicating that the significantly elevated expression level of mutant C2 was the main factor underlying the enhanced lactulose production in the selected hyper-producing strain (Fig.

Interestingly, for C2E mutant C2, the $\sim$ 32-fold increase in expression level seemed not to be due to its higher codon usage bias, which is thought to affect expression levels via the abundance of various tRNA molecules $^{34,35}$ .
The mutant C2E enzyme with \~32-fold enhanced expression level was selected, demonstrating the high efficiency of the lactulose biosensor.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Expression increase observed; need to rule out codon bias.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Is the expression increase due to codon optimization?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Codon usage bias is not responsible for the expression increase.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Analyze codon usage of C2 mutant.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Increased expression not due to codon usage bias; mechanism not fully elucidated.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Limitation noted; overall biosensor validation remains.

evidence.quote_or_span: Interestingly, for C2E mutant C2, the $\sim$ 32-fold increase in expression level seemed not to be due to its higher codon usage bias, which is thought to affect expression levels via the abundance of various tRNA molecules $^{34,35}$ .

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Does the biosensor concept work despite limitations?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: The whole-cell biosensor successfully enabled high-throughput screening, demonstrating potential for engineering LacI for various small-molecule sensors.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Conclude on biosensor utility and note limitations.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Biosensor enabled selection of C2 mutant with ~32-fold expression increase; moderate induction (2.7-fold), weak affinity (KD=17 mM), and IPTG response remain limitations.

evidence.quote_or_span: The mutant C2E enzyme with \~32-fold enhanced expression level was selected, demonstrating the high efficiency of the lactulose biosensor.
As a whole-cell biosensor, strain BLGR harboring plasmid pLac7-L5 was successfully used for high-throughput screening of the C2E mutagenesis library for lactulose hyper-producers.
LacI-L5 has been successfully applied as a lactulose biosensor in engineering cellobiose 2-epimerase (C2E) $^{32}$ for enhanced lactulose synthesis efficiency.
Half-maximal induction occurs at 0.13 mM lactulose.

success_verification.final_conclusion: LacI-L5 biosensor enabled high-throughput screening of C2E library, yielding a mutant with ~32-fold expression increase and 22-fold production increase, though with moderate induction, weak affinity, and residual IPTG response.
limitations: C2E improvement mainly from expression, not catalytic efficiency
Mechanism of expression increase not fully elucidated
